Oil prices surged above $100, while the United Nations warned that a potential Iran war could push 32 million people into poverty. Gold prices also dipped following the escalation in the U.S.-Iran conflict.

story_clusters.summary: India is accelerating the rollout of piped natural gas (PNG) and increasing the supply of 5-kg LPG cylinders amid disruptions in West Asia and Middle East supply chains. The country is also deepening energy ties with the U.S., focusing on nuclear and LPG cooperation due to concerns over Iran-linked supply issues. Additionally, Indraprastha Gas Limited (IGL) is expanding PNG connections in the Delhi-NCR region, targeting 5,000 daily new connections.

perspectives.unique_angle: Homebuyer Cannot Retain Recovery Amount Due to Priority Rules Under IBC: NCLT Allahabad The Tribunal held that allowing retention of the amount would amount to preferential recovery

story_clusters.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that the NCLT should not examine whether a "pre-existing dispute" will succeed when considering a Section 9 application by an operational creditor under the IBC. Additionally, the Court held that investments of multi-state co-operative societies must align with their own business as per bye-laws. The NCLT Allahabad also ruled that homebuyers cannot retain recovery amounts due to priority rules under the IBC.

articles.summary: The Women's Reservation Bill, which proposes to reserve one-third of seats in Parliament and state legislatures for women, will be taken up during the extended Budget Session. The government's decision to include it in the session indicates progress in the legislative process.

articles.title: RBI cancels the licence of National Urban Cooperative Bank Ltd., Pratapgarh
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI), vide order dated April 10, 2026, has cancelled the licence of  National Urban Cooperative Bank Ltd., Pratapgarh , under Section 22 read with Section 56 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949 (BR Act). Consequently, the bank ceases to carry on banking business, with effect from the close of business on April 10, 2026. The Commissioner and Registrar of Cooperative, Uttar Pradesh has also been requested to issue an order for winding up the bank and appoint a liquidator

articles.title: TCS Not Applicable on Illegal Mining Fines Due to Absence of Transfer of Rights: SC
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that Tax Collected at Source (TCS) does not apply to compounding fees for illegal mining activities because these fines do not involve the transfer of rights through lease or license. The court clarified that TCS liability under Section 206C(1C) only arises when rights are legally transferred. This decision impacts how illegal mining penalties are taxed and sets a precedent for similar cases.
articles.raw_summary: The issue was whether TCS applies to compounding fees collected from illegal mining activities. The court held that Section 206C(1C) applies only where rights are transferred through lease or licence. The key takeaway is that absence of contractual or legal rights excludes TCS liability. The post TCS Not Applicable on Illegal Mining Fines Due to Absence of Transfer of Rights: SC appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Department of Expenditure had issued a D.O. letter to Chief Secretaries of States, as an advisory to states and not a directive, to align their bonus policy to promote pulses, oilseeds and millets, in line with the national priorities for nutritional security, Aatmanirbharta (self-reliance) and sustainable agriculture
articles.summary: The Department of Expenditure issued a Departmental Order (D.O.) letter to Chief Secretaries of states as an advisory, not a directive, urging them to align their bonus policies to promote pulses, oilseeds, and millets. articles.title: Constructive Res Judicata Applies to Title Claims Not Raised Earlier: Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that if a title claim was not raised in an earlier lawsuit, it cannot be pursued in a later suit under the principles of constructive res judicata and Order II Rule 2 of the Civil Procedure Code. This decision impacts how property disputes are litigated in India, preventing parties from raising new title claims in subsequent cases if they were omitted earlier. The ruling clarifies the scope of res judicata, ensuring finality in litigation and preventing abuse of the judic
articles.raw_summary: Supreme Court rules that the omission of a title relief claim in an earlier suit bars later claims under constructive res judicata and Order II Rule 2 of the CPC.

articles.title: India-UK free trade pact may come into force from second week of May: Official
articles.summary: The India-UK free trade agreement is set to begin in the second week of May. This pact will allow 99 percent of Indian exports to enter Britain duty-free. articles.title: Govt proposes overhaul of company incorporation rules to cut paperwork, enable risk-based checks

The changes aim to reduce paperwork, minimize duplicate filings, improve electronic communication, and align incorporation processes with GST and Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code (IBC) regulations. This move is intended to streamline procedures for businesses while enhancing risk-based checks during incorporation.

articles.title: Gujarat HC quashes GST proceedings over notices sent to old address despite update
articles.summary: The Gujarat High Court invalidated GST proceedings against a taxpayer because tax notices were sent to an outdated registered address despite the taxpayer updating the address. The Court ruled that proper service of notices is essential for legal validity and adherence to natural justice. This decision underscores the importance of following correct procedures in tax administration.
articles.raw_summary: Failure to serve notices at the updated registered address invalidates GST proceedings. The Court emphasized strict adherence to proper service requirements and natural justice. articles.title: Executing Court Bound to Execute Decree as Passed; Can’t Modify Its Terms: Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that executing courts cannot modify the terms of a decree once passed, even if practical challenges arise such as land exchanges being impracticable due to constructions or prior sales. The court emphasized that such reasons are immaterial to the execution process. This decision underscores the finality of court decrees and limits judicial discretion during enforcement.
articles.raw_summary: Merely for the reasons that exchange of some portions of the land may not be practicable for the reason that constructions on it are not as per the sanctioned map or that part of it has been sold off, are all immaterial.  The post Executing Court Bound to Execute Decree as Passed; Can’t Modify Its Terms: Supreme Court appeared first on SCC Times .

The framework is part of India's MSME policy to ensure timely payments to small businesses.

articles.title: Investments Of Multi State Co-Operative Societies Must Align With Society's Own Business As Per Bye-Laws: Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that multi-state co-operative societies can only invest in companies that are their subsidiaries or operate in the same line of business, as per their bye-laws and Section 64 of the Multi-State Co-operative Societies Act, 2002. The bench clarified that such investments, including under the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code, 2016, must align with the society's own business activities. The ruling restricts investment flexibility for these societies.

articles.title: Co-operative Society can only submit resolution plan for corporate debtor in same line of business
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that a Multi-State Co-operative Society can only submit a resolution plan for a corporate debtor operating in the same line of business, as per Section 64(d) of the Multi-State Co-operative Societies Act, 2002. This means such societies are ineligible to propose resolution plans for corporate debtors in unrelated sectors. The decision impacts the participation of co-operative societies in the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code (IBC) resolution process.
articles.raw_summary: Supreme Court held that section 64(d) of the Multi-State Co-operative Societies Act, 2002 restricts Multi-State Co-operative Society investment only to entities in the same line of business. Accordingly, Multi-State Co-operative Society is ineligible to submit resolution plan for corporate debtor which doesn’t operate in same line of business. The post Co-operative Society can only submit resolution plan for corporate debtor in same line of business appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Service Tax Demand Set Aside as Confirmed Under Taxable Category not proposed in SCN
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal ruled that tax authorities cannot change the taxable category during adjudication if it was not originally proposed in the show cause notice (SCN). The tribunal set aside a service tax demand because it exceeded the scope of the notice. The ruling underscores that the SCN is the foundation for tax demands and cannot be altered later. This decision impacts how tax authorities frame and pursue tax demands in India.
articles.raw_summary: The case addressed whether authorities can change the taxable category during adjudication. The Tribunal ruled that the show cause notice is the foundation and cannot be altered. The demand was set aside as it exceeded the scope of the notice. articles.summary: The Customs, Excise and Service Tax Appellate Tribunal (CESTAT) ruled that goods confiscated as prohibited can now be cleared for domestic use if a beneficial policy amendment is applied retrospectively. The tribunal held that such amendments apply retroactively, permitting home consumption after payment of applicable duties. The decision impacts customs and tax regulations for previously confiscated goods.

articles.summary: The Kerala High Court permitted two transgender individuals to continue hormone therapy, stating that abruptly stopping such treatment could lead to 'absurd results.' This ruling underscores the judiciary's role in protecting vulnerable groups' access to essential healthcare. The case highlights ongoing legal and policy debates around transgender rights in India.

The dispute involves the government, judiciary, and civil society groups advocating for gender justice. The outcome could influence future interpretations of constitutional principles and women's rights.

articles.title: Customs Demand Set Aside Due to Failure to Prove Suppression Despite Valuation Dispute
articles.summary: A customs tribunal set aside a demand for duty evasion after the department failed to prove intent to suppress or evade taxes, despite a dispute over the valuation of imported goods. The decision impacts how customs authorities assess and enforce duty evasion cases in India.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that although inclusion of freight and insurance was legally justified, the department failed to establish suppression or intent to evade duty. Consequently, the extended limitation period was held inapplicable and the demand was quashed. articles.title: What could come after a rules-based world order?
articles.summary: The conflict between the US, Israel, and Iran has raised concerns about the stability of the post-World War II rules-based global order. Analysts suggest this could signal a shift in international governance, prompting questions about what system might replace it. The implications of such a transition could affect global diplomacy, trade, and security frameworks that India relies on.
articles.raw_summary: The war between the US, Israel and Iran was, for many, a sign that the rules-based world order, a set of principles developed after World War II, is collapsing. If that era is over, what could be next?

articles.title: Minor missing for 8 months: Delayed FIR, HC orders removal of Bokaro cop
articles.summary: A minor has been missing for eight months in Bokaro, and the High Court has ordered the removal of a police officer for delayed filing of the First Information Report (FIR). The court's directive highlights systemic issues in law enforcement response to missing persons cases, particularly involving vulnerable groups. The case underscores the need for accountability in police procedures and timely action in child protection matters.

articles.title: Punjab Cabinet clears bill proposing life term for ‘sacrilege’ of Guru Granth Sahib
articles.summary: The Punjab Cabinet has approved a draft amendment to state law proposing life imprisonment for the sacrilege of the Guru Granth Sahib. The move follows multiple incidents that the government states have deeply hurt public sentiments and caused unrest. The bill will now proceed through the legislative process. The amendment reflects the state's response to religious sensitivities and public order concerns.
articles.raw_summary: The draft amendment to the law follows several incidents that have ‘deeply hurt public sentiments’ and caused unrest, the government said.

articles.title: Orphaned by the pandemic, let down by government
articles.summary: Six years after the COVID-19 pandemic, children who lost parents to the virus continue to struggle with education and financial support. Government schemes at both central and state levels have failed to provide adequate assistance, leaving these children in distress. The article highlights systemic gaps in policy execution and the urgent need for intervention to address their welfare and educational rights.
articles.raw_summary: Six years after Covid-19 epidemic, several children continue to struggle for education and money as the implementation of state and central schemes falters.

articles.title: GST Refund Allowed on Scrap as It Qualifies as Manufactured Good Under Scheme
articles.summary: A court ruled that GST refunds cannot be denied on scrap if it qualifies as a manufactured good under the GST scheme. Authorities had previously rejected refunds by treating scrap as non-specified goods, but the court found this interpretation inconsistent with the scheme's rules. The ruling emphasizes that benefits cannot be withheld based on grounds not supported by official notifications. The decision impacts businesses dealing with scrap materials and clarifies GST refund eligibility.
articles.raw_summary: Authorities denied GST reimbursement on scrap treating it as non-specified goods. The court found this interpretation inconsistent with the scheme’s definition and exclusion list. The key takeaway is that benefits cannot be denied on grounds not supported by the notification. The post GST Refund Allowed on Scrap as It Qualifies as Manufactured Good Under Scheme appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Govt Raises SAED on Diesel Exports to Rs 24/Litre
articles.summary: The Indian government amended an excise duty notification to increase the Special Additional Excise Duty (SAED) on diesel exports to ₹24 per litre. The change, effective immediately, aligns with recent duty revisions and affects petroleum product taxation. The government cited public interest as the reason for the revision.
articles.raw_summary: The government amended an earlier excise notification to revise the applicable rate to ₹24 per litre. The change has been made in public interest and takes effect immediately. articles.title: Service Tax Not Applicable on Freight Margins Due to Principal-to-Principal Transactions
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal ruled that margins earned from buying and selling cargo space are trading profits, not service taxable income, as these transactions are conducted on a principal-to-principal basis. The ruling clarifies that such freight margins are not liable for service tax. The decision impacts businesses involved in logistics and freight services by providing clarity on tax obligations for principal-agent transactions.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that margins earned from buying and selling cargo space are trading profits, not consideration for services. Such transactions on a principal-to-principal basis are not liable to service tax. The post Service Tax Not Applicable on Freight Margins Due to Principal-to-Principal Transactions appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Why activists see the imprint of Hindutva in the new transgender law
articles.summary: Activists argue that India's new transgender law primarily recognizes identities within Hindu society while excluding others. The law, which is a government policy, is criticized for cutting off paths to legal recognition for a range of transgender individuals. Activists see the influence of Hindutva ideology in the law's framing and are calling for broader inclusion.
articles.raw_summary: The law foregrounds trans identities that lie broadly within Hindu society, and cuts off paths to recognition for a range of others.

articles.title: Valuation Issues alone were not valid ground for detention of goods in transit in IGST movement
articles.summary: A legal ruling has clarified that valuation issues alone cannot justify detaining goods in transit under the Integrated Goods and Services Tax (IGST) regime. The decision, based on Sections 129 and 130 of the IGST Act, limits the grounds for interception to matters examinable at that stage. This impacts tax enforcement and compliance for businesses moving goods across states in India.
articles.raw_summary: Valuation issues alone were not valid grounds for detention of goods in transit in Integrated Goods and Services Tax (IGST) movement, as such matters could not be examined at the stage of interception under Sections 129 and 130. The post Valuation Issues alone were not valid ground for detention of goods in transit in IGST movement appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Assam order seeking deployment of forest protection force for polls suffers from illegality: NGT
articles.summary: The National Green Tribunal (NGT) has ruled that an Assam government order to deploy a forest protection force for elections is illegal. The NGT found the order lacked legal validity, raising concerns over the misuse of forest protection personnel for poll-related duties. This decision highlights tensions between electoral processes and environmental governance in Assam.

articles.title: At ICA conference, CJI Surya Kant underscores arbitration’s role in global economy
articles.summary: Chief Justice of India (CJI) Surya Kant stated at an arbitration conference that the term 'Alternative Dispute Resolution' may need to be reconsidered, as arbitration is no longer an alternative but a primary method of resolving disputes. The CJI emphasized arbitration's growing importance in the global economy and its role in reducing litigation burdens. The remarks were made during a discussion on the evolving landscape of dispute resolution in India.
articles.raw_summary: Chief Justice Kant expressed that, “it may be time to rethink the very expression ‘Alternative Dispute Resolution’ since it is no longer an alternative to litigation”.

articles.title: Sabarimala Day 3: Govt urges ‘desi lens’ for Articles 25-26, says SC orders relied on Western definition
articles.summary: A nine-judge Supreme Court bench led by CJI Surya Kant is hearing a reference on the interplay between Articles 25 and 26 of the Indian Constitution, which deal with religious freedom. The government has urged the court to adopt an 'Indian lens' for interpreting these articles, arguing that the Supreme Court's previous orders relied on Western definitions. The case has significant implications for religious practices and constitutional rights in India.
articles.raw_summary: The interplay between Articles 25 and 26 is central to the Sabarimala reference, which a nine-judge Supreme Court bench led by CJI Surya Kant is hearing.

articles.summary: The Supreme Court stated that the right to vote and contest elections are not fundamental rights under the Constitution. articles.title: GST on Expense Reimbursement: Pure Agent, Valuation, Invoicing & GSTR-1 Compliance

The piece also outlines invoicing and GSTR-1 compliance requirements for businesses handling reimbursements.

articles.title: OpenAI releases child safety policy framework recommendations to combat AI-enabled CSAM
articles.summary: OpenAI released a policy framework in April 2026 to address AI-generated child sexual abuse material (CSAM). The recommendations include legal reforms, stronger reporting systems, and safety-by-design measures to combat the misuse of generative AI. The framework aims to close gaps in protecting children from online exploitation. The issue has significant implications for AI regulation and child welfare policies globally, including India.

articles.title: Gujarat HC raps CBDT for delay in ITR forms & utility release from last 11 years
articles.summary: The Gujarat High Court criticized the Central Board of Direct Taxes (CBDT) for failing to release Income Tax Return (ITR) forms and their utility tools for the past 11 years. The Court ordered the CBDT to file an affidavit explaining these delays and shortcomings in the e-filing system. This ruling highlights systemic issues in tax administration that could impact taxpayers and policy implementation. The case underscores the need for timely regulatory action in India's tax governance.
articles.raw_summary: The case focuses on systemic delays and technical shortcomings in the e-filing system. The Court directed the Department to file an affidavit addressing these concerns. The post Gujarat HC raps CBDT for delay in ITR forms   utility release from last 11 years appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Deduction u/s. 10A entitled before setting off carry forward losses and unabsorbed depreciation
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) in Agra ruled that an assessee is entitled to claim a deduction under Section 10A of the Income Tax Act before setting off carry-forward losses and unabsorbed depreciation. The assessee's appeal was allowed on this ground. The ruling clarifies the priority of tax deductions in corporate filings, impacting how businesses compute their taxable income under Indian tax law.
articles.raw_summary: ITAT Agra held that the assessee is entitled to claim deduction u/s. 10A of the Income Tax Act before setting off carry forward losses and unabsorbed depreciation. Accordingly, appeal of the assessee succeeds on this ground. The post Deduction u/s. 10A entitled before setting off carry forward losses and unabsorbed depreciation appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Reopening Invalid as Based on Issue Already Decided in Earlier Year: Gujarat HC
articles.summary: The Gujarat High Court ruled that reopening a tax case based on an issue already decided in an earlier year is invalid. The case involved a disallowance under Section 80IB(10), which had already been reversed by appellate authorities. The court held that reopening on the same ground was not permissible. This decision impacts tax administration and legal precedents in India.
articles.raw_summary: The case examined reopening based on a prior disallowance under Section 80IB(10). The Court found that the disallowance had already been reversed by appellate authorities. Therefore, reopening based on the same ground was held invalid. The post Reopening Invalid as Based on Issue Already Decided in Earlier Year: Gujarat HC appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: IRDAI asks insurers to review digital platforms, ensure compliance with dark pattern guidelines
articles.summary: The Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India (IRDAI) has directed all insurers to audit their digital platforms for compliance with dark pattern guidelines issued by the Central Consumer Protection Authority (CCPA). Insurers must submit compliance reports within 15 days and action plans within one month. This move aims to protect consumers from deceptive practices in digital insurance services.

articles.title: No Section 4A Duty on Tobacco Pouches Under 10g for Lack of MRP Requirement
articles.summary: The Customs, Excise and Service Tax Appellate Tribunal ruled that tobacco pouches weighing under 10 grams are exempt from Section 4A excise duty because they lack a Maximum Retail Price (MRP) requirement. This exemption is based on Rule 34 of valuation norms. The decision impacts how small tobacco products are taxed under India's excise duty regime.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that tobacco pouches under 10 grams are exempt from MRP-based valuation under Rule 34. As a result, Section 4A excise duty was not applicable. The post No Section 4A Duty on Tobacco Pouches Under 10g for Lack of MRP Requirement appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Mere Recovery of Weapon Insufficient if No Link to Crime Established: SC
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that mere recovery of a weapon under Section 27 of the Evidence Act is insufficient to convict an accused unless there is a clear link established between the weapon and the crime. The court acquitted the accused due to a lack of a complete chain of evidence connecting the recovered weapon to the offence.

articles.title: So, Here's the Problem: Textile Factories in 10 Nations Face Inconsistent Environmental Permit Rules, UN Study Finds
articles.summary: A UNEP report found that environmental permitting systems in ten major textile-producing nations, including India, have significant gaps in regulating industrial pollution from textile factories. Most national laws do not classify textile activities as environmentally significant or consider gendered impacts on workers. The study highlights inconsistencies in how spinning mills, dye houses, and garment factories are regulated, raising concerns about worker safety and environmental compliance.
articles.raw_summary: Environmental permitting systems across ten major textile-producing nations contain significant gaps in how industrial pollution from spinning mills, dye houses and garment factories is regulated, a new UNEP report has found. Most national legislation fails to classify textile activities as environmentally significant or to consider gendered impacts on workers.

articles.title: Customs Refunds & Unjust Enrichment: Export Proceeds vs FOB Value, Burden of Tax & Legal Tests
articles.summary: The article explains that customs refunds in India are only granted if the claimant proves the duty burden was not passed on to others. Courts have denied refunds and credited the Consumer Welfare Fund when this burden cannot be established. The discussion revolves around export proceeds versus FOB value, tax burden, and legal tests under customs law.
articles.raw_summary: Refunds under customs law are allowed only when the claimant proves the duty burden was not passed to another. Courts have held that failure to establish this results in denial of refund and credit to the Consumer Welfare Fund. The post Customs Refunds   Unjust Enrichment: Export Proceeds vs FOB Value, Burden of Tax   Legal Tests appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Homebuyer Cannot Retain Recovery Amount Due to Priority Rules Under IBC: NCLT Allahabad
articles.summary: The National Company Law Tribunal (NCLT) in Allahabad ruled that a homebuyer cannot retain recovery amounts from an insolvency estate due to priority rules under the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code (IBC). The tribunal directed the refund of the amount to the insolvency estate and limited the homebuyer's claim to the admitted amount, citing that allowing retention would constitute preferential recovery. The ruling impacts how homebuyers recover dues during insolvency proceedings under the IBC.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that allowing retention of the amount would amount to preferential recovery. It directed refund to the insolvency estate and limited the claim to the admitted amount. The post Homebuyer Cannot Retain Recovery Amount Due to Priority Rules Under IBC: NCLT Allahabad appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Iran war could plunge 32 million into poverty, says United Nations
articles.summary: A United Nations report warns that a war involving Iran could push 32 million people worldwide into poverty due to rising energy and food costs and weaker economic growth. The UN Development Programme describes this as a 'triple shock' affecting energy, food, and economic stability. The report highlights that developing countries, including India, may face the worst impacts.
articles.raw_summary: ‘Development in reverse’ taking place involving rising energy and food costs and weaker economic growth More than 32 million people worldwide could be plunged into poverty by the economic fallout from the Iran war, with developing countries expected to be hit hardest. In a report issued amid doubts over a fragile ceasefire, the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) said the world was facing a “triple shock” involving energy, food and weaker economic growth. Continue reading...

articles.summary: Kerala faces a growing fiscal crisis with rising debt and declining tax revenue, described by opposition leaders as a 'ventilator' economy. The state's ability to fund salaries and pensions is strained, while external factors like the West Asia crisis could further hurt consumption. Opposition leaders argue populist policies may not survive a shrinking treasury, raising questions about future governance and economic stability.

articles.title: What are Karnataka’s AI-focussed responsible social media, digital safety rules?
articles.summary: Karnataka is proposing a new digital safety bill to regulate social media platforms. The bill aims to use AI for content moderation, require labels for synthetic content, and enforce faster removal of harmful posts. It prioritizes user safety, especially for younger users, and includes stricter timelines and institutional oversight for compliance. The bill reflects broader efforts to address digital risks in India's growing online ecosystem.

User safety, especially for younger audiences, stricter timelines and institutional oversight to enforce compliance are among its other aims.

This initiative aims to strengthen India's EV value chain and reduce dependency on imports.

articles.summary: The Indian government has increased export duties on diesel and aviation turbine fuel (ATF) to ₹55.5 per litre and ₹42 per litre, respectively. The hike is part of the windfall tax adjustments announced by the Centre.

The project is expected to have significant implications for water management and infrastructure development in the region.

The situation highlights potential risks to energy markets and supply chains, which could impact India's energy imports and pricing.

The move follows threats by former US President Trump and could disrupt global oil supplies. Crude oil prices have already surged back above $100 per barrel in response. India, a major oil importer, may face energy price volatility and supply chain disruptions as a result.
articles.raw_summary: US Central Command has said that they will begin blocking ships entering and exiting the Strait of Hormuz from Monday 10 AM Eastern Time, but ships headed to non-Iranian ports will not be blocked.

articles.title: West Bengal Polls 2026: TMC says EC chief asked delegation to 'get lost', ECI shares ultimatum
articles.summary: The Trinamool Congress (TMC) alleged that Chief Election Commissioner Gyanesh Kumar told their delegation to "get lost" during discussions on voter deletions in West Bengal. The Election Commission of India (ECI) denied any bias and asserted that the state polls will be conducted fairly. The TMC's claim highlights concerns over electoral processes ahead of the 2026 West Bengal elections. The ECI has not yet responded to the allegations beyond denying them.

articles.title: ‘Apology would set terrible precedent’: Kunal Kamra tells Maharashtra council panel
articles.summary: Comedian Kunal Kamra appeared before a Maharashtra council panel investigating his social media posts. The panel suggested that an unconditional apology could change how they view the matter. Kamra refused, arguing that an apology would set a bad precedent. The interaction highlights ongoing debates about free speech and government oversight of online content.
articles.raw_summary: This came after the committee told the comedian that it would ‘look at the matter differently’ if he were to express unconditional remorse.

articles.title: Russia tempts energy-starved South Asia with 40% discounts on US-sanctioned LNG
articles.summary: Russia is offering a 40% discount on US-sanctioned liquefied natural gas (LNG) to energy-starved South Asian countries, including India. This move could reshape regional energy trade dynamics and impact India's energy import strategies amid global supply constraints. The offer comes as India seeks to diversify its energy sources to meet growing demand.

articles.title: India to push for reporting of offshore rupee trades despite resistance, says report
articles.summary: India plans to push ahead with a proposal requiring banks to report offshore rupee derivative trades, aiming to improve transparency and ease pressure on the currency. The RBI has proposed that lenders share data on at least 70% of such transactions from February 2027, despite resistance from foreign banks over regulatory concerns.
articles.raw_summary: India plans to push ahead with a proposal requiring banks to report offshore rupee derivative trades, aiming to improve transparency and ease pressure on the currency. The RBI has proposed that lenders share data on at least 70% of such transactions from February 2027, despite resistance from foreign banks over regulatory concerns.

articles.summary: India has withdrawn its bid to host the UN’s COP33 climate summit in 2028, reversing an offer made by Prime Minister Narendra Modi at COP28 in Dubai. The government has not cited a reason.

The ruling reinforces limits on the misuse of criminal law. This decision impacts how courts handle cases where evidence contradicts initial accusations, ensuring fairer legal processes. The judgment is significant for preventing abuse of the criminal justice system.

articles.title: Urgent cases to be mentioned only before CJI-led bench: SC
articles.summary: The Supreme Court (SC) has ruled that urgent cases must now be mentioned only before a bench led by the Chief Justice of India (CJI), even if the CJI is unavailable or presiding over a constitution bench. This changes the usual practice where urgent matters were handled by the seniormost available judge. The decision impacts how cases are prioritized and listed for hearing at the apex court.
articles.raw_summary: According to the usual practice, urgent matters are mentioned for listing and hearing before the seniormost judge of the apex court if the CJI is either unavailable or busy heading a constitution bench.

articles.title: Andhra Pradesh plans law to regulate social media access for children under 13
articles.summary: The Andhra Pradesh government is planning to introduce a law to regulate social media access for children under 13. The proposed law includes exploring age verification mechanisms, tightening action against online abuse, and promoting digital safety education in schools. This initiative aims to protect children from potential harms associated with social media use. The state is considering these measures as part of broader efforts to enhance child welfare and digital governance.

articles.title: Government raises gas supply to fertiliser plants, boosts fuel allocation amid West Asia tensions
articles.summary: The Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas (MoPNG) has raised gas supply to fertilizer plants by 5%, reaching around 95% of their six-month average consumption starting April 9, 2026. This decision is based on available inventory and scheduled LNG cargo arrivals amid tensions in West Asia. The move aims to ensure adequate fuel supply for fertilizer production, which is critical for India's agricultural sector.
articles.raw_summary: The Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas (MoPNG) has increased gas allocation to fertilizer plants by 5% to around 95% of their six-month average consumption, effective April 9, 2026, based on available inventory and scheduled LNG cargo arrivals.

articles.title: Advait Energy emerges lowest bidder for DGVCL turnkey power project tender
articles.summary: Advait Energy has been selected as the lowest bidder for a turnkey power project tender issued by DGVCL. The project involves supplying and installing 11KV medium voltage covered conductors under the Vanbandhu Kalyan Yojana-2 (VKY-2) scheme, aimed at tribal welfare. This is part of a government-backed initiative to improve power infrastructure in tribal areas.
articles.raw_summary: The tender pertains to the supply, installation, testing and commissioning of 11KV 55mm² AAAC Medium Voltage Covered Conductor (MVCC), along with accessories and additional poles if required, under the Vanbandhu Kalyan Yojana-2 (VKY-2) scheme or other schemes.

articles.title: DMRC begins mid-life refurbishment of Blue Line trains, 70 trains to be upgraded in phases
articles.summary: The Delhi Metro Rail Corporation (DMRC) has begun refurbishing 70 trains across the Red and Blue Lines in phases. In the first two phases, 30 trains have already been upgraded, with work on another 18 trains set to start soon. The third phase will cover 22 Blue Line trains by November 2027. This initiative aims to modernize and extend the lifespan of the metro fleet, improving public transportation infrastructure in Delhi.
articles.raw_summary: DMRC is refurbishing 70 trains across the Red and Blue Lines in a phased manner. In the first phase, 12 trains have already been upgraded, while 18 trains were refurbished in the second phase. Work on another nine trains is set to begin soon, followed by an additional nine trains. In the third phase, 22 Blue Line trains will be refurbished by November 2027.

articles.title: YSRCP push ‘Mavigun’ capital proposal aggressively

articles.title: Cancer courses through the Ghaggar
articles.summary: Villages along Haryana’s Ghaggar river, particularly Sirsa and Fatehabad, report a rise in cancer cases allegedly linked to contaminated water. Local political representatives have raised the issue in Parliament and the State Assembly, demanding urgent government action. However, tackling the crisis is hindered by a lack of reliable cancer data in the state and inadequate treatment facilities, especially in Sirsa.

articles.title: From diversity to monotony, ecological communities are homogenising
articles.summary: The article explores biotic homogenisation, where diverse ecosystems worldwide are losing unique species and becoming increasingly similar due to environmental changes. This process replaces sensitive endemic species with common generalist ones, raising concerns about ecological balance and biodiversity loss. The phenomenon is documented globally but has implications for India's environmental policies and conservation strategies.
articles.raw_summary: Imagine walking through different ecosystems across the world, only to find that species inhabiting them look strikingly similar. This is the reality of biotic homogenisation, a process where diverse biological communities become increasingly similar as unique endemic species, highly sensitive to environmental changes, are replaced by common generalist species, which are insensitive to such perturbations. [ ]

articles.summary: According to a study by the Wildlife Institute of India (WII), dolphins require a minimum depth of three metres in flowing rivers to survive during lean seasons. The WII study highlighted that the decreasing flow of the Chambal River has been affecting dolphin movement. During the lean season, dolphins tend to concentrate in the deeper pools of the river. The WII’s study analysed field data on the distribution of Ganges River dolphins, gharials, and other important aquatic life, including turtle

The WII’s study analysed field data on the distribution of Ganges River dolphins, gharials, and other important aquatic life, including turtle

articles.title: AERA cuts domestic flight landing and parking charges by 25% for 3 months
articles.summary: The Airport Economic Regulatory Authority (AERA), India's airport tariff regulator, has reduced domestic flight landing and parking charges by 25% for three months across major airports. This decision aims to ease operational costs for airlines amid economic challenges. The move is expected to benefit both airlines and passengers by lowering expenses. AERA is the body responsible for setting airport tariff regulations in India.
articles.raw_summary: Airport tariff regulator Airport Economic Regulatory Authority (AERA) has announced a 25% reduction in landing and parking charges across major airports.

articles.title: I do not see the Assembly election in Tamil Nadu as a close contest, says CM Stalin
articles.summary: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister M.K. Stalin stated that the upcoming Assembly election is not a close contest, framing it as a battle between the DMK-led alliance and the AIADMK-led front. He highlighted the DMK's achievements and electoral promises as key factors in the people's support. Stalin also expressed confidence that the DMK can win two consecutive elections, countering skepticism about their electoral prospects.
articles.raw_summary: The contest is essentially between the DMK-led front and the AIADMK-led alliance. Our achievements and electoral promises have captured the imagination of the people. The outcome will disprove the notion that the DMK cannot win two consecutive elections, says the DMK president

articles.title: Early signs of Alzheimer’s: Struggling with daily tasks
articles.summary: The article explains that chronic struggles with daily tasks over months or years may indicate early disruptions in the brain's ability to coordinate complex activities, potentially signaling the onset of Alzheimer's disease. It highlights the importance of recognizing these persistent difficulties as possible early symptoms of a neurodegenerative condition.
articles.raw_summary: Chronic struggles that linger or worsen over months and years may reveal early disruptions in the brain’s ability to co-ordinate complex tasks.

The expansion is being led by the government-backed Digi Yatra Foundation.

articles.title: NTPC arm commissions 250 MW of Tehri pumped storage project
articles.summary: NTPC's subsidiary has commissioned the fourth unit of the 1,000 MW Tehri pumped storage project (PSP) in Uttarakhand, marking the commercial operation of this unit. The project is part of THDC India Limited's (THDC) PSP and supports NTPC's broader 20 GW pumped storage pipeline. Pumped storage projects are critical for grid stability and renewable energy integration in India's energy transition.
articles.raw_summary: With the commissioning the fourth unit of THDC’s 1,000 MW Tehri PSP enters commercial operations as NTPC advances its 20 GW pumped storage pipeline.

articles.title: US-Iran agree to two-week ceasefire brokered by Pakistan, invited to Islamabad for further talks
articles.summary: The United States and Iran have agreed to a two-week ceasefire in their six-week conflict across West Asia, brokered by Pakistan. Both countries have been invited to Islamabad for further talks to address the ongoing tensions. This development could impact regional stability, which is significant for India's foreign policy and security interests.
articles.raw_summary: Twin announcements by US President Trump   Iranian Foreign Minister Araghchi bring six-week long conflict across West Asia to temporary halt.

articles.title: Government plans weekly system to track EXIM trends
articles.summary: The Indian government plans to set up a weekly system to track export-import trends and industry stress, led by the commerce ministry. This move aims to address challenges faced by exporters, such as rising packaging costs and shipping disruptions, which are affecting sectors like apparel, leather, and medical devices. The initiative is part of efforts to streamline cargo handling procedures amid global tensions and cost pressures threatening domestic businesses.
articles.raw_summary: The govt will establish a system to monitor export-import trends and industry stress weekly. This comes as global tensions and rising costs threaten domestic businesses. The commerce ministry is working to streamline procedures for cargo handling. Discussions focused on challenges faced by exporters, including packaging material costs and shipping disruptions. Industries like apparel, leather, and medical devices are particularly vulnerable.

articles.raw_summary: The announcement of the ceasefire will not determine the trajectory ahead, but by what follows within its limited duration. Two weeks is not a resolution horizon. It is a decision window.

articles.title: Net FDI inflows dip, investors opting for Mexico, Vietnam
articles.summary: India's net foreign direct investment (FDI) inflows have declined as investors increasingly favor Mexico, Poland, and Vietnam for nearshoring and friendshoring strategies after the Covid pandemic. Experts attribute this shift to these countries' competitive advantages and the US securing a large share of tech-related investments. The trend raises concerns about India's attractiveness as an investment destination compared to its peers.
articles.raw_summary: Officials and experts attribute this trend to the country having to compete with Mexico, Poland, and Vietnam, a preferred part of investors' nearshoring and friendshoring policies after the Covid pandemic, and the US cornering a large share of tech-related investments.

articles.summary: Anthropic’s new AI model, Mythos, has prompted rare, coordinated alerts from major central banks worldwide, including the U.S. Federal Reserve and European Central Bank, warning of potential cyber risks posed by advanced algorithms. The model’s capabilities have raised concerns about systemic vulnerabilities in financial systems, as AI-driven threats could emerge as a new frontier for systemic risk alongside traditional market risks. articles.title: MP HC Appoints Shourya Didis for 19-Year-Old Woman Who Left Matrimonial Home After Marriage to 40-Year-Old Man, Allows Her to Live With Lover
articles.summary: The Madhya Pradesh High Court appointed 'Shourya Didis' (sisters-in-law) to support a 19-year-old woman who left her matrimonial home after marrying a 40-year-old man. The court allowed her to live with her lover, stating she was a major and had chosen this relationship due to mistreatment by her husband. The decision highlights judicial intervention in personal disputes with implications for women's rights and protection under civil liberties.
articles.raw_summary: The Woman informed the Court that she was a major and was living with Respondent 4 by choice as her husband was much older and ill-treated her. articles.title: Where Relationship Ends In A Breakup, The Man Is In The Lockup: Karnataka HC
articles.summary: The Karnataka High Court quashed criminal proceedings against a man accused of wrongdoing after a relationship ended, ruling that a failed relationship cannot be criminalized without proof of deceit at its inception. The court emphasized that the absence of criminal intent at the start of the relationship invalidates subsequent legal action. This ruling sets a precedent for how relationship disputes are treated under criminal law in India.
articles.raw_summary: The Court held that a failed relationship cannot be criminalised without proof of deceit at inception. It quashed proceedings citing absence of criminal intent. The post Where Relationship Ends In A Breakup, The Man Is In The Lockup: Karnataka HC appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Recyclable Materials Mean Nothing if the Product Cannot Be Disassembled
articles.summary: Performance outerwear, due to its complex multi-material construction and bonded components, is nearly impossible to recycle. A new research project is testing whether redesigning products for easier disassembly could solve this challenge. The apparel industry's technical complexity has long made end-of-life recovery difficult, but this initiative aims to change that from the design stage. The outcome could inform future policies on sustainable fashion and textile waste management.
articles.raw_summary: Performance outerwear is among the most technically complex categories in apparel—and among the hardest to recycle. Multi-material construction, bonded membranes, and inseparable trims have long made end-of-life recovery a structural impossibility rather than a logistical inconvenience. A new research and development project is testing whether design for disassembly can change that calculus from the ground up.

articles.title: The Sourcing Story: Industry Rewrote Its Geography Without Rewriting Its Dependence
articles.summary: The global garment industry has relocated apparel assembly to new countries over the past decade due to trade tensions and compliance pressures, but key inputs like fabrics and fibres remain concentrated in China. New research shows how production networks have reconfigured without reducing dependence on Chinese industrial inputs. This shift could impact India's role in global supply chains, given its growing manufacturing sector and trade policies. The findings underscore the need for India to
articles.raw_summary: The global garment industry has spent a decade reconfiguring its production geography in response to trade tensions, compliance pressures, and the demands of fast-moving online retail. New research now provides a detailed account of what has changed and what has not. Apparel assembly has moved, measurably and deliberately. The industrial inputs that make that assembly possible—fabrics, fibres, accessories—have stayed in China.

articles.title: Global sea surface temperatures are reaching record highs [Commentary]
articles.summary: Global sea surface temperatures (SST) are reaching record highs, driven by climate change. Oceans absorb most of the planet's excess heat, but the consequences of rising SST—such as marine ecosystem disruptions and extreme weather—are often overshadowed by discussions on air temperatures. This trend underscores the urgent need for climate action, including policy measures to address ocean warming and its impacts.
articles.raw_summary: Rising Sea Surface Temperatures (SST) and their seasonal fluctuations have emerged as among the most visible indicators of a warming planet. Yet, while public discourse on global warming tends to focus on rising air temperatures driven by anthropogenic greenhouse gas emissions, the increase in SST and its profound consequences remain underemphasised. articles.title: ADB raises India’s FY27 growth forecast to 6.9%
articles.summary: India's economic growth forecast for FY27 has been raised to 6.9 percent by the Asian Development Bank. This upward revision is driven by increasing consumption, higher investment, supportive government policies, and recent trade pacts. While FY26 is projected at 7.6 percent, FY27 anticipates a moderation due to global uncertainties. The outlook for FY28 is a robust 7.

articles.title: US forces to blockade ships entering, exiting Iranian ports after no breakthrough in Islamabad talks
articles.summary: The US Central Command (Centcom) announced plans to blockade ships entering or exiting Iranian ports, stating that vessels from all nations would be affected. The move follows stalled talks in Islamabad and could disrupt maritime trade routes in the region. This action may influence global oil supplies and trade dynamics, with potential implications for India's energy imports and foreign policy considerations.
articles.raw_summary: US Centcom said that vessels of no nation will be spared from the blocked as it will be imposed impartially.

articles.title: US sending forces to help with mine-clearing in Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: The US is sending forces to assist with mine-clearing operations in the Strait of Hormuz. President Trump stated that the deployment is underway regardless of ongoing diplomatic talks. The strait is a vital shipping route for global oil supplies, including those bound for India. The move could impact regional stability and energy markets, with potential consequences for India's crude oil imports.
articles.raw_summary: “We are sweeping the strait. Whether we make a deal or not makes no difference to me,” Trump said as talks extended into early Sunday morning.

articles.title: J&K LG terminates services of two government employees over terror links
articles.summary: Jammu and Kashmir Lieutenant Governor Manoj Sinha terminated the services of two government employees for their alleged links with terror outfits, including Lashkar-e-Taiba and Hizbul Mujahideen. The decision highlights the government's stance on countering terrorism and internal security threats. The employees' alleged affiliations with proscribed organizations raise concerns about national security and governance in the region.
articles.raw_summary: Jammu and Kashmir Lieutenant Governor Manoj Sinha on Wednesday terminated the services of two government employees for their alleged links with terror outfits, including LashkareTaiba and Hizbul Mujahideen, official sources said.

articles.title: Aga Khan Foundation director writes on securing sustainable rural water supply services
articles.summary: India’s progress in expanding rural drinking water access in recent years has been remarkable. Yet the true success of these efforts will be measured not merely by the number of connections installed, but by whether safe and reliable water supply becomes an accepted norm of rural life, notes Asad Umar, Director-WASH Health, Aga Khan Foundation India, Rural WASH Partner Forum (RWPF).

articles.title: Himanta wife passport row: Telangana HC gives Pawan Khera 1-week protection, tells him to move Assam court
articles.summary: The Telangana High Court has granted Pawan Khera, a Congress leader, a one-week transit anticipatory bail in a defamation and criminal conspiracy case. The case was filed over Khera's claims about Assam Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma's wife holding multiple passports. The court directed Khera to approach the Assam court for regular anticipatory bail. The incident highlights tensions between political figures and raises questions about defamation laws and free speech.
articles.raw_summary: Defamation, criminal conspiracy case against Khera lodged over his ‘multiple-passport’ claims against Assam CM's wife. HC has allowed transit anticipatory bail.

The crisis stems from geopolitical tensions affecting fuel supply chains, with potential implications for India's energy imports and aviation sector.

The development could impact India's energy costs and economic policies, given the country's heavy reliance on oil imports.

articles.title: The Iran War Has Exposed Taiwan’s Achilles’ Heel
articles.summary: The article examines Taiwan's defensive preparations for a potential conflict with China, highlighting features like conscription, hardened infrastructure, and natural barriers. It compares Taiwan's defences to a porcupine's tough exterior. The analysis is framed around Taiwan's strategic challenges in the event of war, with no direct connection to India's policy landscape.
articles.raw_summary: In the event of a war with China, Taiwan has some surprisingly tough defenses that could make it as difficult to tackle as a porcupine: A shoreline dotted with swamps, rocks, and concrete barriers; conscription for all adult men; highways and airports that are built to double as hardened combat facilities.

articles.title: The three market outcome scenarios modelled by JPMorgan on the US-Iran war
articles.summary: JPMorgan has modeled three potential market outcomes based on a US-Iran war scenario. A bullish outcome would involve de-escalation in West Asia or a US Federal Reserve policy shift, leading to a broad market rally, lower bond yields, falling oil prices, tighter credit spreads, and a weaker US dollar. The scenarios are speculative and depend on geopolitical developments and central bank actions.
articles.raw_summary: A bullish scenario will be when there is proper de-escalation in West Asia or there is a policy pivot by the US Federal Reserve. In such a scenario, they see a broad "everything rally" where equities advance, bond yields drop, oil prices fall, credit spreads tighten and the US Dollar weakens.

This situation significantly alters the global economic outlook.

articles.title: AI models like Anthropic's Mythos pose disruption risks to India's IT services growth: Kotak
articles.summary: AI models such as Anthropic's Mythos could pose disruption risks to the growth of India's IT services sector, according to a report by Kotak Institutional Equities. The report said the model "exhibits a step-jump in benchmark performance across software engineering tasks," and added that it "raises near- to medium-term disruption risks for IT services," particularly for companies with higher exposure to application services.

articles.title: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman issues 'ring of power' warning on AGI
articles.summary: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman warned about the societal risks of artificial general intelligence (AGI) and the intense competition among AI companies to control its development. He emphasized that AGI should not be monopolized by a small group of firms. The remarks underscore concerns about unchecked AI advancement and its potential global impact, including for India's policy landscape.

articles.title: Mythos AI alarm bells: Fair warning or marketing hype?
articles.summary: Anthropic has delayed the release of its advanced AI model, 'Claude Mythos,' which is highly skilled in coding and could potentially be exploited by hackers for malicious purposes. The move has sparked discussions about the risks of embedding AI models in malicious code to infiltrate networks. Experts warn that such tactics could become common in cyberattacks, raising concerns about AI safety and regulation.

Meyers saw embedding a tiny AI model directly into malicious code infecting networks as a natural tactic to be explored by hackers.

The bridge signifies grassroots determination overcoming bureaucratic delays.

articles.title: Census 2027 self-enumeration explained: Steps to complete it easily
articles.summary: The Census of India 2027 will introduce a self-enumeration option, allowing individuals to complete their census forms online via the official portal without waiting for an enumerator. This initiative aims to streamline data collection and improve efficiency in the decennial census process. The move is part of the government's digital governance efforts to modernize administrative procedures.
articles.raw_summary: With Census 2027, you don’t have to wait for someone to come home; you can just fill it out yourself online by visiting the official census self-enumeration portal.

articles.title: Explainer: What is in Iran’s 10-point ceasefire plan and will the US agree to it?
articles.summary: The US and Iran agreed to a two-week ceasefire, with Iran temporarily reopening the Strait of Hormuz. The agreement followed pressure from Donald Trump and involved negotiations with Pakistan and China. Israel also joined the ceasefire. The plan aims to ease tensions in the Middle East, a region critical for global oil supplies.

articles.title: Strait of Hormuz blockade explained: What Trump's new US Navy move means for ships, cargo and Iran
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump announced a naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, a key shipping route for global oil supplies. The move, involving the US Navy, aims to counter Iran's actions in the region. This could disrupt oil shipments and affect India's energy imports, given its reliance on Middle Eastern crude. The Strait handles about 20% of the world's oil supply.
articles.raw_summary: President Donald Trump on Sunday announced that he is imposing a naval blockade on the Strait of Hormuz

articles.title: What is a naval blockade and how would it work in Strait of Hormuz?
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump has announced plans to impose a naval blockade in the Strait of Hormuz, a vital shipping route for global oil supplies. The Strait is a critical chokepoint through which nearly one-third of the world's seaborne oil passes, including significant volumes destined for India. A blockade could disrupt oil flows, potentially leading to supply shortages and price volatility, which would have major implications for India's energy security and economy.
articles.raw_summary: Donald Trump says that the US is going to start blockading the Strait of Hormuz. What does this mean in practice?

articles.title: Mint Explainer | Can AI find bugs humans can’t? Inside Anthropic’s Project Glasswing
articles.summary: Anthropic’s Project Glasswing aims to use advanced AI to detect hard-to-find software vulnerabilities. Backed by Big Tech, the initiative may reshape how software is secured and could eventually disrupt cybersecurity firms and IT services models.
